paper_title: Separation and recovery of Co(II) and Li(I) from spent lithium-ion mobile phone batteries

entities.description: First author and correspondence author of the research paper.
Second author, master student in Theoretical and Physical Chemistry.

entities.id: College of Natural Sciences, Can Tho University
entities.description: Academic institution in Vietnam where the research was conducted.
The main field of research involving metal recovery through chemical aqueous processes.

entities.id: Recovery of Co(II) and Li(I) from spent lithium-ion batteries
entities.description: The primary research problem involving extracting valuable metals from electronic waste.
The hazardous electronic waste material targeted for metal recycling.
Acid leaching process using Hydrochloric acid to dissolve metals from battery powder.
Chemical process used to separate Cobalt using oxalic acid at specific pH.
Method using Trioctylamine (TOA) to selectively extract Co(II) over Li(I).
Commercial extractant used in the solvent extraction process.
Reagent used to precipitate Co(II) from the leaching solution.
Inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometers used for metal concentration measurement.

entities.id: Optimum leaching conditions (4 M HCl, 50oC, 1.5 hrs)
entities.description: Best parameters identified for the acid leaching process.
The percentage of cobalt successfully dissolved into the acid solution.
The percentage of lithium successfully dissolved into the acid solution.
The ideal acidity level for recovering cobalt as cobalt oxalate.
The low percentage of lithium loss during the cobalt precipitation phase.
